footnote: Titles count the whole Spanish women's top flight back to 1988–89 — Liga Nacional, División de Honor, Superliga, Primera División and Liga F are one continuous championship. Never-champions count from their club's own first top-flight match. The 16 clubs shown are the 2026–27 season's.

teams.name: Deportivo Alavés
teams.firstGameNote: Beat Real Betis 2–1 at tiny Ibaia in their 2021 debut, in the club's centenary year; relegated in 2023, the Gloriosas return for 2026–27 — runners-up to a Barcelona B side that couldn't be promoted.

lastTitle.clinch: clinched without playing when Barcelona fell 0–1 to Atlético, a day after Athletic's own 3–0 over Oviedo Moderno — champions by a single point

teams.name: Atlético de Madrid

lastTitle.clinch: won 3–1 at Real Sociedad on the final day — an Esther González hat-trick — for a third straight title

teams.lineage: The 1990 title was won by Atlético Villa de Madrid, the original women's section, dissolved in the early 1990s and refounded in 2001; the club's own honours list counts three.

teams.name: FC Badalona Women
teams.firstGameNote: Won 2–1 at Real Betis in the current era's opener — a Mari Paz Vilas brace, after a referee strike wiped out matchday one; born in a Sant Joan Despí barrio, the club first tasted the top flight in 2012 and has worn three names in three years.

lastTitle.clinch: won 4–1 in the derby at Espanyol — Graham Hansen and a Fenger brace — to seal a seventh straight title with four matchdays to spare

teams.name: Costa Adeje Tenerife
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 3–1 to Barcelona in their 2015 debut in Granadilla; the island club flies to every away game, has never been relegated, and 2026's fourth place is the high-water mark.

teams.name: Deportivo Abanca
teams.firstGameNote: Beat Espanyol 3–1 at Abegondo in their 2019 debut, bringing top-flight women's football back to A Coruña 31 years after the great Karbo Deportivo dissolved.

teams.name: DUX Logroño
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 0–0 with Valencia in Las Gaunas' first-ever women's Primera match in 2018, having won promotion by beating CD Tacón — the club Real Madrid would buy a year later.

teams.name: SD Eibar
teams.firstGameNote: Beat Real Betis 1–0 at Unbe in SD Eibar's own 2020 top-flight debut; the lineage runs deeper — as Eibartarrak they reached the top flight in 1997 and finished league runners-up in 2001.

lastTitle.clinch: won 2–0 at Puebla on the final day while rivals Híspalis were held 3–3 at Athletic — the title flipped on the head-to-head tiebreaker

teams.name: Granada CF
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 4–1 at Levante in their 2013 debut and went straight back down; a decade passed before the return, and 2025's fifth place is the club record.

teams.name: Madrid CFF
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 1–1 at Levante in their 2017 debut as the 42nd club to reach the Spanish top flight — a fully independent club founded in 2010 with one of Spain's biggest girls' academies, never relegated since.

teams.name: Real Madrid
teams.firstGameNote: Lost the first-ever Clásico femenino 0–4 to Barcelona at Valdebebas in their 2020 debut; five runner-up finishes in six seasons since — every one behind Barcelona — and still no trophy of any kind.

teams.name: Real Sociedad
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 2–2 with Atlético at Zubieta in their 2006 debut after the federation handed them a vacated spot; the 2019 Copa de la Reina remains the lone major trophy.

teams.name: Sevilla FC
teams.firstGameNote: Won 3–1 at Jaén in the club's own 2009 top-flight debut, two years after restarting from Seville's provincial leagues — the 2006 near-title belonged to partner club Híspalis wearing their colors.

teams.name: Valencia CF
teams.firstGameNote: Lost the derby 0–2 to Levante in their 2009 debut, weeks after absorbing a team born in a German school's playground; relegated in 2025, they return via a 99th-minute play-off equalizer.
